slides.title: Your cells have a *self-cleaning mode*. Here's the switch.
slides.sub: It's called autophagy — the recycling system tied to how well you age. You can nudge it on.

slides.title: Cellular garbage collection
slides.body: Autophagy clears out *damaged cell parts* and recycles them for fuel. Less junk inside your cells.

slides.title: It fades with age
slides.body: This cleanup *slows down as you age* — and sluggish autophagy is linked to a lot of age-related decline.

slides.title: Time without food
slides.body: A simple *12–16 hour overnight fast* is the most reliable everyday nudge for this pathway.

slides.title: Eat spermidine foods
slides.body: Wheat germ, aged cheese, mushrooms, and natto are rich in *spermidine*, a compound that supports it.

slides.title: Move and sweat
slides.body: Exercise and heat *may switch it on* too. You don't need extremes — just consistency.

slides.title: Build the rhythm that *ages you well*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your eating window, food, and movement in one place — so the habits behind healthy aging actually stick.

caption:
Your cells have a built-in cleanup crew called autophagy — and it slows down as you age. 🧬

The everyday levers are simple: a 12–16 hour overnight fast, spermidine-rich foods like mushrooms and wheat germ, plus movement and heat. 🔖

Vibe Health tracks your eating window and habits in one place → link in bio.
youtube_title: Autophagy: How to Switch On Your Cells' Self-Cleaning Mode
